name: reflex-ai

metadata.name: reflex-ai
metadata.summary: AI tools to build Reflex apps.

documentation:
# Reflex-AI

`reflex-ai` is a package to bring AI agent capabilities to your local development environment.

It also includes blocks for higher level components.

## 1. Install the Package

Install the package with pip:

```bash
pip install reflex-ai
```

NOTE: `reflex-ai` requires `reflex` 0.6.0 or later. Before this is out, make sure to run the following:

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/reflex-dev/reflex.git@main
pip uninstall watchfiles
```

## 2. Create a Reflex Project

`reflex-ai` works with both new and existing Reflex projects. To create a new project, create a directory for your app and run:

```bash
reflex init
```     

See the [reflex docs](https://github.com/reflex-dev/reflex) for more information on creating Reflex projects.

## 3. Enable AI

To enable AI in your project, add the following to your main app file.

```bash
# Import the reflex-ai package (you MUST import it exactly like this)
from reflex_ai import enable

app = rx.App()
# Enable AI BEFORE you call any `add_page`.
enable(app)

# Add your pages as usual.
app.add_page(index)
```

## 4. Run the App

Run your app as usual:

While we're in beta, you need to take the following steps:

Export the backend URL for the AI agent:
```
export FLEXGEN_BACKEND_URL=https://rxh-prod-flexgen-agent.fly.dev/
```

Install `

Then run your app as usual:

```bash
reflex run
```

NOTE: You must be logged in to Reflex to use `reflex-ai`.

## 5. Access the AI Editor at `/edit`

Every page in your app will have an additional route by adding `/edit` to the end of the URL. For example, if your index page is at `localhost:3000`, you can access the AI editor at `localhost:3000/edit`. Similarly, if your about page is at `localhost:3000/about`, you can access the AI editor at `localhost:3000/about/edit`.

When `reflex-ai` is enabled, a scratch copy of your app will be created in the `.web/reflex-ai-tmp` directory for the AI to make changes without affecting your main app.

## 6. Select and Edit Elements

On an edit page, you can select elements by clicking on them. The selected element will be highlighted in the editor and the toolbar will show the selected code.

You can enter prompts in the toolbar input to generate new content for the selected element. The AI will generate new content based on the prompt and update the element in the editor.

### Caveats

functions.name: reflex_ai.scripts.validate_source.noop
documentation:
A no-op function.

Args:
    *args: The arguments to ignore

functions.name: reflex_ai.scripts.validate_source.get_custom_import
documentation:
Get a custom import function that disables reflex_ai.enable,
and replaces imports of the modified file the code living in new_source_code_file.

Args:
    modified_file: The name of the modified file.
    new_source_code_file: The new source code file where the changes are made.

Returns:
    The custom import function.

functions.name: reflex_ai.scripts.validate_source.validate_source
documentation:
Validate the source code by running the validation function.

Args:
    new_source_code_file: The new source code file where the changes are made.
    modified_file: The name of the modified file.
    validation_function: The validation function to run.
    validation_file: The file containing the validation function.

Raises:
    Exception: If the code being validated raises an exception.

modules.name: reflex_ai.scripts.get_module_script
documentation: Script to get the source file of a module in a sandbox.

functions.name: reflex_ai.scripts.get_module_script.custom_import
documentation:
A custom import to disable reflex_ai.enable.

Args:
    name: The name of the module to import.
    globals: The global namespace.
    locals: The local namespace.
    fromlist: The list of names to import.
    level: The level in the package hierarchy.

Returns:
    The imported module.

functions.name: reflex_ai.scripts.get_module_script.get_module
documentation:
Import the module from the scratchpad.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module to import.
    root_dir: The root directory of the module.
    scratch_dir: The scratch directory of the module.

Returns:
    The imported module.

modules.name: reflex_ai.local_agent
documentation: The agent that runs on the user's local client.

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.InternRequest
documentation: The request to the AI intern.
NOTE: using BaseModel here instead of rx.Base due to FastAPI not liking the v1 models

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.InternResponse
documentation: The response from the AI intern.

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.ToolRequestResponse
documentation: The response from the tool to the AI intern.

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.EditResult
documentation: The result of an edit.

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.Diff
documentation: A diff between two files (before and after).

classes.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent

methods.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent.authorization_header
documentation:
Construct an authorization header with the specified token as bearer token.

Args:
    token: The access token to use.

Returns:
    The authorization header in dict format.

methods.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent.make_request
documentation:
Make a request to the backend.

Args:
    endpoint: The endpoint to send the request to.
    data: The data to send.
    timeout: The timeout for the request.

Returns:
    The JSON response from the backend.

methods.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent.process
documentation:
Process the request from the AI intern.

Args:
    request: The request from the AI intern.

Yields:
    The tool use messages from the intern.

methods.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent._handle_tool_result
documentation:
Handle the tool response.

Args:
    tool_result: The result from the tool.
    tool_call: The tool call item.
    request: The original request.

Returns:
    The processed tool response.

methods.name: reflex_ai.local_agent.LocalAgent.confirm_change
documentation:
Accept or reject the change.

Args:
    thread_id: The thread ID.
    diffs: The diffs to accept or reject.
    accepted: Whether the change is accepted or rejected.
The local agent instance.

functions.name: reflex_ai.views.bottom_bar.bottom_bar
documentation: Bottom bar

methods.name: reflex_ai.runtime.RunTimeState.reset_scratch_dir
documentation:
Reset the scratch directory on every reflex run.

Args:
    base_path: The root path of the client working dir.
Perform some actions on app startup.

modules.name: reflex_ai
documentation: The local Reflex agent.

functions.name: reflex_ai.enable
documentation:
Enable the agent on an app.

Args:
    app: The app to enable the agent on.

Note:
    For now, this must be called before add_page is called as
    we override the add_page method.

Raises:
    Exception: If watchfiles is installed

classes.name: reflex_ai.EditableState

methods.name: reflex_ai.EditableState.get_name
documentation:
Get the name of the state.

Returns:
    The name of the state.
Rewrite rx.State imports in copied modules to this new class to avoid conflicts.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.ast_utils.import_module_from_scratch
documentation:
Import the module from the scratchpad.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module to import.
    root_dir: The root directory of the module.
    scratch_dir: The scratch directory of the module.

Returns:
    The imported module.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.ast_utils.get_module_source
documentation:
Get the file path of a module in a subprocess.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.

Returns:
    The source code of the module.

Raises:
    Exception: If the subprocess fails.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.ast_utils.get_module_ast
documentation:
Get the AST of a module.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.

Returns:
    The AST of the module.

modules.name: reflex_ai.selection
documentation: Handle point-and-click element selection in a Reflex app

functions.name: reflex_ai.selection.get_selection_props
documentation:
Generate on_click handler and outline props for selectable components.

Args:
    selection: The selected component.

Returns:
    A dictionary of props that includes the on_click handler and outline props.

functions.name: reflex_ai.selection.component_create_override
documentation:
Generate an override for Component.create that is active for the given paths.

Args:
    base_paths: Components originating from modules prefixed by these paths will be clickable.

Returns:
    A replacement Component.create function that adds an on_click handler where needed.

functions.name: reflex_ai.selection.clickable
documentation:
A decorator helper to make all components in a given page clickable to select.

The active selection (filename and line range) is stored in
ClickSelectionState.selected_element

The code for the selection is cached in ClickSelectionState._selected_code

Args:
    base_paths: Components originating from modules prefixed by these paths will be clickable.

Returns:
    A decorator that adds an on_click handler to the component.

classes.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection.get_selection_id
documentation:
Get a unique identifier for the selection.

Returns:
    A unique identifier for the selection.

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection.get_selection_code
documentation:
Get the code for the selection.

Returns:
    The code for the selection.

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection.get_selection_code_filename_and_line_range
documentation:
Get the filename and line range for the selection.

Returns:
    The filename and line range for the selection.

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection.get_selection_module
documentation:
Get the module for the selection.

Returns:
    The module for the selection.

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.Selection.detect_ide
documentation:
Detect the current IDE based on running processes

Returns:
    The name of the IDE.
Capture details of a selected component.

classes.name: reflex_ai.selection.ClickSelectionState

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.ClickSelectionState.handle_click
documentation:
Handle a click event on a component.

Args:
    selection: The selected component.

methods.name: reflex_ai.selection.ClickSelectionState.click_page
documentation:
Given a file, and the "main component" of the file, handle a click event on the component.

Args:
    filename: The file to open
    function: The "main component" of the file
Track the currently selected component and the code that created it.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.get_ast_and_source
documentation:
Get the AST and source code of a module.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.

Returns:
    The AST and source code of the module.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.find_target_node
documentation:
Find the target node in the AST.

Args:
    tree: The AST of the module.
    element_type: The type of element to find (function, class, or variable).
    element_name: The name of the element to find.
    class_name: The name of the class to search within (if applicable).

Returns:
    The target node in the AST.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the class or element is not found.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.generate_diff
documentation:
Generate the diff between the previous source and the new tree.

Args:
    previous_source: The previous source code.
    new_tree: The new AST.

Returns:
    The diff between the previous source and the new tree.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.to_unified_diff
documentation:
Convert a list of diff lines to a unified diff string.

Args:
    ndiff: The list of diff lines.

Returns:
    The unified diff string.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.add_python_element
documentation:
Add a new element (class, function, or variable) to a Python file after the imports.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.
    content: The content of the new element.
    class_name: The name of the class to add the element to (if applicable).

Returns:
    The diff between the previous source and the new source.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the class is not found, or if we try to add in a duplicate element

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.update_python_element
documentation:
Update the source code of an element (class, function, or variable) in a module.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.
    element_type: The type of element to update (class, function, or variable).
    element_name: The name of the element to update.
    new_content: The new content of the element.
    class_name: The name of the class containing the element (if applicable).

Returns:
    The diff between the previous source and the new source.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the element is not found.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.codegen.delete_python_element
documentation:
Delete an element (class, function, or variable) from a module.

Args:
    module_name: The name of the module.
    element_type: The type of element to delete (class, function, or variable).
    element_name: The name of the element to delete.
    class_name: The name of the class containing the element (if applicable).

Returns:
    The diff between the previous source and the new source.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the class or element is not found.

modules.name: reflex_ai.toolbar
documentation: The local AI toolbar to hook up with the server-side reflex agent.

classes.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState

methods.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState.process
documentation:
Process the user's prompt.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt from the user from the form input.

Yields:
    Updates to the frontend.

methods.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState.confirm_change
documentation:
Accept the current diff.

Args:
    accepted: Whether the diff is accepted or not.

methods.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState.trigger_reload
documentation: Trigger a hot reload.

class_variables.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState.current_step
documentation: The current step name of the process (selected_code, processing, review_changes)

class_variables.name: reflex_ai.toolbar.ToolbarState.selected_diff
documentation: The selected diff
The toolbar state.

modules.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils
documentation: Utility functions for the reflex_ai package.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.ignore_anything_but_rxconfig
documentation:
This is a helper function which tell sh.copytree to ignore the .web directory from being copied, if it exists

Args:
    dir: The directory being copied (not used but necessary for the spec)
    contents: The contents of the directory

Returns:
    A list of directories to ignore (just .web if it exists)

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.convert_to_scratch_code
documentation:
Convert reflex app code to code that would belong in the scratch directory.

For now:
    - Import EditableState from reflex_ai
    - convert rx.State to EditableState
    - add "tmp" to the front of the names of in-app imports

Args:
    app_name: The name of the app
    code: The code to convert

Returns:
    The converted code

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.convert_from_scratch_code
documentation:
Performs the exact inverse of the above function: converts code from the scratch directory to code belonging in the original app.

Args:
    app_name: The name of the app
    code: The code to convert

Returns:
    The converted code

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.get_scratch_dir
documentation:
Get the location of the scratch directory where the agent makes changes.

Args:
    app_dir: The directory of the app that was copied into the scratch directory.

Returns:
    The path to the scratch directory.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.create_scratch_dir
documentation:
Create a scratch directory for the agent to make changes to.

Args:
    app_dir: The directory of the app to copy into the scratch directory.
    overwrite: Whether to overwrite the scratch directory if it already exists.

Returns:
    The path to the created directory.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.modify_scratch_directory
documentation:
Modify Python files in the scratch directory to use the correct state class.

Args:
    directory: The directory containing files to modify.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.modify_scratch_file
documentation:
Modify the content of a single file in the scratch directory.

Args:
    file_path: The path to the file to modify.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.commit_scratch_dir
documentation:
Copy all files from the scratch directory back to the corresponding app directory.

Args:
    app_dir: The original app directory to copy files back to.
    files: The list of files to copy back.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.format_code
documentation:
Format the code using black.

Args:
    code: The code to format.

Returns:
    The formatted code.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.diff_directories
documentation:
Diff two directories and return the diffs for all Python files.

Args:
    dir1: The first directory.
    dir2: The second directory.

Returns:
    A dictionary mapping file paths to their diffs.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.directory_diff
documentation:
Diff the scratchpad and the base directories.

Returns:
    A dictionary mapping file paths to their diffs.

Raises:
    AssertionError: If the paths are not properly configured.

functions.name: reflex_ai.utils.path_utils.validate_source
documentation:
Validate the diff and write the changes to the file.

NOTE: This function will run all validation processes within the *main* app directory.

Args:
    source: The source code to validate.
    modified_filename: The name of the file to write the changes to.
    validation_function: The name of the validation function to run.
    validation_filename: The name of the file containing the validation function.
